channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kiharu MP Ndindi Nyoro Opens Mwai Kibaki Secondary School in Gakurwe
item.description: Kiharu Member of Parliament Ndindi Nyoro has officially opened a new senior secondary school in Gakurwe, Murang'a County, to honour the late former president Mwai Kibaki. The school was built using fu...

item.title: Government Approves Major Railway Expansion and International Status for Madurai Airport
item.description: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA), chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, has sanctioned two major railway infrastructure projects in eastern India, worth approximately ₹4,474 crore. ...

item.title: Philippines seeks Portugal’s assistance to bring back Zaldy Co facing corruption charges
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. confirmed that the Philippine government is coordinating with Portuguese authorities to facilitate the return of former legislator Zaldy Co, who is facing multiple corru...

item.title: South Africa Records Steady Economic Growth in 2025
item.description: The South African government has expressed satisfaction with the latest data from Statistics South Africa, which shows the country's economy expanded by 1.1% in 2025. The fourth quarter of the year, f...

item.title: Bam Adebayo scores 83 points, setting NBA second-highest single-game record
item.description: In an extraordinary NBA performance, Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo scored 83 points against the Washington Wizards, setting the second-highest single-game point total in league history. The 28-year-ol...

item.title: Anambra court sentences security operative to death for killing phone repairer
item.description: For Ogbaru, Anambra State, High Court don sentence one vigilance group man, Onyeka Eze, to death by hanging after dem find am guilty of killing one phone repairer, Anayo Ugbo, for Okpoko Market. Accor...

item.title: FCT Minister Approves Promotion of Traditional Chiefs in Abuja
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, don approve say all second-class chiefs go become first-class, and all third-class chiefs go become second-class for the FCT Council of Chie...

item.title: Finance Minister Holds Meeting with Dangote Cement to Discuss Investments and Industrial Growth in Tanzania
item.description: On 10 March 2026, the Minister of Finance, Mr Khamis Mussa Omar, met with a delegation from Dangote Cement in Tanzania at the Ministry's satellite offices in Dar es Salaam. The delegation was led by t...

item.title: IHC directs Advocate General to facilitate Imran Khan’s power of attorney for Aitzaz Ahsan
item.description: On Tuesday, the Islamabad High Court (IHC) directed the Advocate General Islamabad to assist in the signing of the power of attorney for senior lawyer Barrister Aitzaz Ahsan by Imran Khan, the founder...

item.title: Corey Feldman responds to exclusion from Oscars tribute for Rob Reiner
item.description: Corey Feldman, known for his role in Rob Reiner’s 1986 film 'Stand By Me', has publicly stated that he was not invited to participate in the Oscars’ In Memoriam segment dedicated to Reiner. The tribut...

item.title: Comelec maintains regular work schedule amid multiple elections in 2026
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that it will not adopt a four-day workweek due to the heavy electoral schedule this year. Comelec Chairman George Garcia explained that with three major...

item.title: Surviving Alone in a Post-Apocalyptic World of Edible Wonders
item.description: In this excerpt from 'Gooday Nagar' by Maithreyi Karnoor, the story unfolds in a post-apocalyptic world where civilisation has ended, yet the remnants are strangely surreal: everything, from vehicles ...

item.title: Tanzanian Businessman Rostam Azizi Takes Majority Control of Nation Media Group After Aga Khan Exit
item.description: The Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) has announced its exit from Nation Media Group (NMG), ending a 66-year involvement with the East African media powerhouse. AKFED sold its 54.08 perce...

item.title: NACC pauses media updates for over two months citing lack of significant news
item.description: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) did not provide any media updates for 63 days, from 10 December to 11 February, citing a lack of 'sufficient newsworthy information.' This hiatus followe...

item.title: Several Liberal MPs may support NDP bill limiting Canadian arms exports to the U.S.
item.description: The NDP's proposed arms-control legislation, known as the No More Loopholes Act (Bill C-233), aims to end largely permit-free Canadian military exports to the United States. If passed, it could see su...

item.title: Court Ruling on Ancestral Land Triggers Dispute in Akpugo, Enugu
item.description: Tension don dey rise for Akpugo, Nkanu West Local Government Area, Enugu State as Umuedenwoko family dey claim say dem never fit access their ancestral farmland even after court don give dem judgment ...

item.title: Pakistan Bloods Young Talent as 2027 ODI Preparations Begin Against Bangladesh
item.description: After a disappointing T20 World Cup campaign, Pakistan cricket team is shifting focus towards the 2027 ODI World Cup. The team is using the upcoming three-match series against Bangladesh at Sher-e-Ban...

item.title: Louis Theroux's 'Inside the Manosphere' documentary review: strengths in approach but shortcomings in addressing effects on women
item.description: In this review of Louis Theroux's 90-minute Netflix documentary 'Inside the Manosphere', the critic acknowledges that Theroux arrives somewhat late to a well-covered subject, following other explorati...

item.title: The impact of floods on public health and governance in Kenya
item.description: Last week’s floods in Kenya reminded us once again that vulnerability to disasters isn’t equal. Poor households, people living in unstable housing, and areas with weak infrastructure are hardest hit. ...

item.title: Evidence Suggests Carrim and Maumela May Have Funded Matlala’s R360m Tender
item.description: At the Madlanga Commission, evidence leader Matthew Chaskalson presented findings suggesting that North West businessman Suliman Carrim and Hangwani Morgan Maumela may have funded Vusi Cat Matlala’s c...
